sourceNotes.caseIdFormat: Matrix case IDs use C001-C030. Runtime assets remain Case_001-Case_030 and are mirrored in assetCaseId.
sourceNotes.unlockEvidenceReferenceFormat: caseLocations.unlockEvidenceIds use globally unique evidence stems such as C003_E001. Integration should down-map to local runtime evidence IDs if writing directly into CaseLocationData.
sourceNotes.legacySuspectPolicy: Cases C001-C010 use authored display-name suspect relevance only. This thread does not create new patrol-era SuspectData assets.

districts.themeSummary: Heritage estates, galleries, old apartment blocks, donor halls, and polished private interiors.
Banks, annexes, records floors, public offices, and precinct-adjacent administration.
Cafes, markets, gyms, clubs, shelters, and high-footfall civilian spaces.
Medical blocks, terminals, public transit edges, and marina-facing movement corridors.
Corporate towers, service cores, and dense office infrastructure.
Storage yards, cold berths, piers, industrial archives, and off-hours logistics.
Trailheads, edge-of-city estates, and secluded approach roads beyond the dense core.

caseArcs.arcSummary: Early patrol cases teach time, access, and physical contradiction reading inside contained city spaces.
Patrol cases widen into cross-town movement, public networks, and the art-theft thread that later feeds Fraud.
A fraud-cleanup network led by Mira Voss rewrites paper trails while an internal leak feeds it restricted timing.
Missing-person cases tighten around rerouted witnesses, dockside handoffs, and the records-desk leak behind the disappearances.

cases.title: The Missing Watch
cases.arcBeatSummary: Introduces patrol-era time contradictions inside a private manor and establishes the city's old-money side as a recurring backdrop.

interrogationUsage.focus: Legacy contradiction-first case. Dossier relevance is present, but interrogation is not prioritized in this milestone.

caseLocations.entryText: Work the study as a sealed high-society scene and teach the player to read time, access, and object placement together.

cases.title: Locked Room Receipt
cases.arcBeatSummary: Moves patrol into controlled institutional access logic and places an early bank case inside the Civic Core.

interrogationUsage.focus: Remains a pure access-log contradiction case.

caseLocations.entryText: Keep the player in one controlled bank space where badge, biometric, and vault timing tell the full story.

cases.title: Coffee Shop Alibi
cases.arcBeatSummary: First authored city-hop contradiction: the cafe alibi fails because the player can compare it against the gallery side of town.

interrogationUsage.focus: Theo Grant is a strong early interrogation-forward suspect because his confidence collapses under travel-time pressure.

caseLocations.entryText: Start at the cafe and let the player collect the receipt and local alibi texture before the contradiction widens.
Second visit verifies the gallery-side timing and reinforces the impossible cross-town alibi.

cases.title: Parking Garage Ticket
cases.arcBeatSummary: Expands patrol into vehicle-placement contradictions while anchoring the medical district on the city map.

interrogationUsage.focus: The ticket and plate-reader contradiction reads cleanly without added suspect pressure.

caseLocations.entryText: Keep the case concentrated around the garage and lobby-story contradiction so the city map gains a clear Riverside hospital node.

cases.title: Stairwell Footprints
cases.arcBeatSummary: Uses weather, footwear, and witness testimony to teach physical contradiction reading in a grittier civilian district.

interrogationUsage.focus: Best kept as an evidence-first scene-reading case.

caseLocations.entryText: Stay inside the stairwell footprint scene so weather and physical trace logic remain tightly legible on mobile.

cases.title: Delivery Window
cases.arcBeatSummary: Begins the second patrol arc by pushing the player onto route-order reasoning at the city's edge.

interrogationUsage.focus: Route contradiction is clear enough without converting it into a suspect-pressure stop.

caseLocations.entryText: Use the estate gate as the anchor scene and let off-screen route evidence do the rest.

cases.title: Neighbor Testimony
cases.arcBeatSummary: Brings patrol back indoors and turns unreliable testimony into a suspect-relevance template for the dossier layer.

interrogationUsage.focus: Useful for dossier flavor, but not promoted to interrogation-forward in this milestone.

caseLocations.entryText: Keep this contradiction centered on one apartment stack so sound, device logs, and witness memory stay tightly paired.

cases.title: Train Platform
cases.arcBeatSummary: Promotes the city network into play: station timing, warehouse geography, and public transit become a clean multi-location contradiction.

interrogationUsage.focus: Carl Duval is a strong station-side interrogation suspect because his confidence depends on public transit detail and bluff.

caseLocations.entryText: Start at the station and let the player build the public-side timeline first.
Second visit grounds the impossible travel distance between the train platform and the warehouse sensor hit.

cases.title: Gym Keycard
cases.arcBeatSummary: Keeps patrol in a civilian venue while showing how schedule systems and back-corridor access reinforce one another.

interrogationUsage.focus: Remains a clean access-and-attendance contradiction.

caseLocations.entryText: Use one gym node and let booking, studio, and staff corridor evidence tell the contradiction without additional travel.

cases.title: Gallery Opening
cases.arcBeatSummary: Patrol capstone that bridges into the later art and fraud network: the gallery alibi breaks once the player compares it to the off-site target location.

interrogationUsage.focus: Simon Rowe is a high-value optional interrogation case because the art-world lie and disappearing public presence suit dossier-driven pressure.

caseLocations.entryText: The gallery remains the launch node and social alibi stage for the case.
A second visit verifies the off-site theft target and pays off the patrol arc's first sustained art-network thread.

cases.title: After-Hours Receipts
cases.arcBeatSummary: Opens Fraud with duplicate paper trails and introduces Mira Voss as a polished outside fixer orbiting donor money.

interrogationUsage.focus: Use dossier presence for Renata and Mira, but keep the case as a document-first onboarding beat.

caseLocations.entryText: Hold the opening fraud case inside one donor-hall scene so players can learn the department's dense document reads cleanly.

cases.title: Kiosk Closeout
cases.arcBeatSummary: Shows Fraud operating in a rougher civilian venue and reintroduces Lyle Mercer as a reusable liar inside the ledger-cleaning ecosystem.

interrogationUsage.focus: Retain the till-and-receipt emphasis without extra interrogation scaffolding.

caseLocations.entryText: The market kiosk should stay single-location so the late reopen and cash handoff remain immediate.

cases.title: False Provenance
cases.arcBeatSummary: Expands the Fraud arc into forged provenance and positions the art trade as a bridge between patrol's gallery thread and Mira's network.

interrogationUsage.focus: Tessa Rook and Owen Kade form a clean dossier-and-pressure pair for early fraud interrogation upgrades.

caseLocations.entryText: Lead from the appraisal room so the player first reads the seal and certificate as a controlled forgery scene.
The alley visit pays off the seal-case movement and turns provenance fraud into a physical handoff problem.

cases.title: Refund Loop
cases.arcBeatSummary: Pushes Fraud upward into tower-space and digital approvals, broadening the department beyond paper without leaving its contradiction DNA.

interrogationUsage.focus: The operations-pod evidence trail is sufficient without elevating Priya to interrogation-forward status this milestone.

caseLocations.entryText: Keep the fintech case inside one operations floor to preserve its tight device-versus-automation logic.

cases.title: Ghost Severance
cases.arcBeatSummary: Shows the fraud network exploiting bureaucratic spaces and quietly foreshadows records-side leakage before it becomes explicit.

interrogationUsage.focus: Archive movement and credentials do the work without extra suspect branching.

caseLocations.entryText: Anchor the case in one bureaucratic archive room where file movement and after-hours edits feel procedural rather than cinematic.

cases.title: Dry Claim
cases.arcBeatSummary: Broadens Fraud into staged physical loss claims while keeping the contradiction legible through material condition, not paperwork alone.

interrogationUsage.focus: A physical-claims case that should stay evidence-forward.

caseLocations.entryText: Keep the flood-claim contradiction in one intake bay so dry-vs-wet evidence remains visually immediate.

cases.title: Split Batch
cases.arcBeatSummary: Returns to paper flow and public funds, showing how the fraud arc can sit inside ordinary treasury operations.

interrogationUsage.focus: Serial gaps and parcel weights already give enough structure.

caseLocations.entryText: One treasury room is enough to teach split-batch fraud without fragmenting the player's paper trail.

cases.title: Restricted Preview
cases.arcBeatSummary: First overt leak beat in Fraud: Mira's timeline depends on restricted knowledge that should never have left the department.

interrogationUsage.focus: This is Mira Voss's first interrogation-priority case because the restricted-brief lie works cleanly as suspect pressure.

caseLocations.entryText: Lead from the conference suite and public invoice packet so the player starts in the clean version of Mira's story.
The warehouse-side verification visit turns abstract warning leaks into visible preemptive movement.

cases.title: Warehouse Float
cases.arcBeatSummary: Brings the ledger-cleaning scheme into industrial collateral space and reconnects Lyle Mercer to the wider network.

interrogationUsage.focus: Use dossier relevance for Mira and Lyle, but keep the storage-locker scene self-contained.

caseLocations.entryText: Treat Bay 14 as a contained fraud bunker so the player reads the lock, access log, and collateral sleeve in one flow.

cases.title: The Glass Ledger
cases.arcBeatSummary: Fraud capstone: Mira stops being a rumor and becomes the exposed fixer who physically moved the original ledger through a compromised annex route.

interrogationUsage.focus: Existing interrogation chain should remain the department's first fully authored suspect-pressure payoff.

caseLocations.entryText: The vault floor is the capstone launch node and should feel like the clean institutional heart of the fraud conspiracy.
The second visit isolates Mira's physical movement through the annex and sharpens the capstone contradiction.

cases.title: Last Stop Window
cases.arcBeatSummary: Opens Missing Persons with a transit-side disappearance and immediately teaches Enhance as a location-truth tool.

interrogationUsage.focus: Start the department with map-and-location reasoning before adding more suspect pressure.

caseLocations.entryText: The terminal should stay one authored node so players learn the Missing Persons map shell without an immediate second hop.

cases.title: Room 3B
cases.arcBeatSummary: Moves the department into temporary housing and keycard chronology, emphasizing who re-entered a supposedly empty room.

interrogationUsage.focus: The room-return lie is strong enough without additional interrogation flow.

caseLocations.entryText: Keep the hostel case inside one building node so the player's first missing-persons indoor chronology is easy to parse.

cases.title: Bluebird Encore
cases.arcBeatSummary: First nightlife disappearance in the arc and the first missing-person beat to visibly reconnect with Mira Voss.

interrogationUsage.focus: Promoter and backstage-witness pressure are strong candidates, with Mira as the wider dossier hook.

caseLocations.entryText: Start backstage so the player's first read is a performance-space departure lie.
The marina-side follow-up confirms Sera left toward the water and carries Mira's shadow into the missing-persons arc.

cases.title: False Dawn Search
cases.arcBeatSummary: Uses field-search optics to show how a missing-person narrative can be manipulated before the official timeline begins.

interrogationUsage.focus: Map travel matters here more than suspect questioning.

caseLocations.entryText: The trailhead remains the public-facing search node where the official story is staged.
A second field visit pays off the predawn movement and makes the fake search window feel spatially real.

cases.title: Stairwell Five
cases.arcBeatSummary: Brings Missing Persons into an office tower and emphasizes Enhance as a truth tool for dead-screen surveillance.

interrogationUsage.focus: The reflected-badge reveal should land as an evidence moment, not an interrogation beat.

caseLocations.entryText: One service-core node is enough to keep the dead-camera reflection trick sharp and mobile-friendly.

cases.title: Cold Berth
cases.arcBeatSummary: Shifts the arc onto the cold docks and shows the missing-person trail moving from locker row into a deeper industrial berth system.

interrogationUsage.focus: Dockside sequence and berth routing matter more than added suspect questioning.

caseLocations.entryText: The locker row is the public-facing dock node and the first place the disappearance is falsely contained.
The deeper berth visit pays off the enhanced cargo tag and makes the dockside abduction route tangible.

cases.title: Quiet Shelter
cases.arcBeatSummary: First overt Missing Persons leak beat: a restricted routing slip appears before the public filing, tying the witness chain to internal records flow.

interrogationUsage.focus: Tova Rell is a strong interrogation-forward suspect because her lie is protective, not purely criminal, which suits the deeper suspect flow.

caseLocations.entryText: Keep the shelter on one map node for now and let the booth reflection, routing slip, and courtyard traces carry the escalation.

cases.title: Pier 19 Shadow
cases.arcBeatSummary: Turns the arc back toward Mira's dockside network and shows a missing-person trail crossing into controlled industrial access.

interrogationUsage.focus: The pier override and barge hint play better as map-and-route evidence than as a first-pass interrogation scene.

caseLocations.entryText: Use one pier node for now so the override reader, radio, and rain-smeared camera remain tightly linked.

cases.title: Silent Draft
cases.arcBeatSummary: Reporter Vera Hale's disappearance turns the leak from rumor into urgent personal evidence and points directly at precinct-side service access.

interrogationUsage.focus: Editor Hal Renn and annex-side support staff are strong optional interrogation targets before the capstone.

caseLocations.entryText: Launch from the newsroom-side desk so the player first reads Vera's own investigative trail.
The service-ramp follow-up confirms Vera physically reached the annex and sharpens the transition into the final records-floor case.

cases.title: The Hollow Desk
cases.arcBeatSummary: Missing Persons capstone: the internal leak is traced to Inez Farrow's desk, connecting the fraud brief chain and the rerouted witness cases through one records-floor operator.

interrogationUsage.focus: Existing interrogation should remain the capstone payoff for the deeper suspect flow and the clearest bridge between dossier, map, and contradiction systems.

caseLocations.entryText: Launch from the records desk and keep the player inside the institutional center of the leak network.
The corridor visit isolates the cleaning-cart reflection and supports the first capstone interrogation beats.
Final visit pays off the moved routing copies and makes the desk-side leak operational rather than abstract.
